title: Unidentified Rebel crew chief
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Conjecture}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:ChiefRimmer-ANH.jpg]] |name=Unidentified Rebel crew chief |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]{{Film|IV}} |gender= |pronouns=He/him{{CCG|set=Special Edition Limited|cardname=Incom Engineer}} |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Graying]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation= *[[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom Corporation]] *[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|This R2 unit of yours seems a bit beat up. You want a new one?|The crew chief, to Luke Skywalker|}} An [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] worked at the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic's]] [[Great Temple/Legends|Great Temple]] base on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], serving as a '''crew chief'''. Initially an employee of the [[Fresia/Legends|Fresia]]-based [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom Corporation]], the [[Imperialization/Legends|nationalization]] of that company by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] prompted the [[Chief/Legends|chief]] and his co-workers to join the Rebellion. In his role with the Alliance, the chief spent many [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] performing maintenance on [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] and [[T-47 airspeeder/Legends|T-47 airspeeders]]. ==Biography== The [[Chief/Legends|chief]] was initially an [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] working for [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom Corporation]], a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] company based on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Fresia/Legends|Fresia]].''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'' During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Imperialization/Legends|nationalized]] the corporation,{{CSWECite|II|99|Incom Corporation}} prompting many workers, such as the engineer, to flee and join the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. Like his former co-workers, he spent many [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] performing maintenance on [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] and [[T-47 airspeeder/Legends|T-47 airspeeders]] in the Alliance's service; both types of craft had initially been made by Incom.''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' The engineer was eventually given the role of [[Crew chief/Legends|crew chief]], began work as a technician, and was assigned to the Alliance's [[Great Temple/Legends|Great Temple]] base{{TCG|set=A New Hope|link=https://swtcg.com/Content/Images/Cards/ANH/Rebel_Crew_Chief.webp?v=2|cardname=Rebel Crew Chief}} on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. [[File:Rimmerandsomeotherdude.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The crew chief and Luke Skywalker]] In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' [[Death Star plans/Legends|technical readouts]] for the Empire's new [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] were brought to the Yavin base by [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], and [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] devised a plan of attack. The attack would involve the T-65s, and so the chief performed maintenance on at least one of them before the battle. The T-65's [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], intended to use the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] as his [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech]] for the upcoming [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|battle]], but the chief noted that the droid did not look to be in the best of shape. He asked Skywalker whether he wanted a replacement, but the pilot declined, claiming that he and R2-D2 had been "through a lot together." Finishing his maintenance, the chief cleared Skywalker for liftoff.{{SWRadio|Force and Counterforce}} Skywalker would go on to destroy the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]]. ==Personality and traits== The crew chief was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] with [[Color/Legends|graying]] hair and light skin. ==Equipment== The chief had a tan-colored [[Clothing/Legends|suit]]. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|Whatever you say.|The crew chief, to Luke Skywalker in dialogue not in ''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope''|}} The crew chief was portrayed by [[Shane Rimmer]]{{YouTube|video=eoeGE_b5fvM|text=SHANE RIMMER Interview - R2D2 was Trouble - Star Wars 100 Interview|channel=channel/UCMQtQzDFjoIcCo5HHbcHd2w|name=
Star Wars 100 Interviews|quote=For Sci Fi Central Australia, Darryl James interviewed Shane Rimmer in Wales. His role in ANH was called an InCom Engineer. Not only was Rimmer in Star Wars buy [''sic''] he was also a key character voice in the original Thunderbirds TV series. SHANE RIMMER Interview - R2D2 was Trouble.}} in the [[1977]] [[original trilogy]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], although his first appearance was in [[Alan Dean Foster]]'s [[1976]] [[Star Wars: A New Hope (novelization)|novelization of the film]]. Some inconsistencies exist between the crew chief's film appearance and various adaptations. The crew chief has slightly different dialogue in the film's novelization,[[Star Wars: A New Hope (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope'' novelization]] along with the [[November 5]], 1977 [[Star Wars (1977) 5|fifth issue]] of [[Marvel Comics]]' [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book series]],[[Star Wars (1977) 5|''Star Wars'' (1977) 5]] [[Brian Daley]]'s [[1981]] [[Star Wars (radio)|radio adaptation]], the [[1994]] [[Star Wars: The National Public Radio Dramatization|''Star Wars'': The National Public Radio Dramatization]], which contains the radio drama's script,[[Star Wars: The National Public Radio Dramatization|''Star Wars'': The National Public Radio Dramatization]] and[[Star Wars: A New Hope - The Special Edition 3|''Star Wars: A New Hope - The Special Edition'' 3]] the [[March 5]], [[1997]]{{GalaxyCite|10|Back to the Drawingboard}} [[Star Wars: A New Hope - The Special Edition 3|third issue]] of [[Bruce Jones]]' comic adaptation. In ''Star Wars'' 5, he says an earlier line to Skywalker before a different version of his film line is delivered, while both the radio drama and its script contain additional dialogue with him responding to Skywalker's lines in the film. ==Appearances== *{{Film|IV}} *[[Star Wars: A New Hope (novelization)|''Star Wars: A New Hope'' novelization]] {{Ab|Star Wars: A New Hope (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *[[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope (junior novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'' junior novelization]] *[[Star Wars: A New Hope - The Special Edition 3|''Star Wars: A New Hope - The Special Edition'' 3]] *[[Star Wars (1977) 5|''Star Wars'' (1977) 5]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 5}} *{{SWRadio|Force and Counterforce}} *''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader]]'' {{Video}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: The National Public Radio Dramatization|''Star Wars'': The National Public Radio Dramatization]] {{ID|Crew Chief}} *{{CCG|set=Special Edition Limited|cardname=Incom Engineer}} *{{TCG|set=A New Hope|link=https://swtcg.com/Content/Images/Cards/ANH/Rebel_Crew_Chief.webp?v=2|cardname=Rebel Crew Chief}} *{{CSWECite|III||Skywalker, Luke}} {{Po}} ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of unidentified Rebel crew chief}} {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{YouTube|video=eoeGE_b5fvM|text=SHANE RIMMER Interview - R2D2 was Trouble - Star Wars 100 Interview|channel=channel/UCMQtQzDFjoIcCo5HHbcHd2w|name=Star Wars 100 Interviews|quote=For Sci Fi Central Australia, Darryl James interviewed Shane Rimmer in Wales. His role in ANH was called an InCom Engineer. Not only was Rimmer in Star Wars buy he was also a key character voice in the original Thunderbirds TV series. SHANE RIMMER Interview - R2D2 was Trouble.}} [[Category:Alliance Military members]] [[Category:Engineers]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified gender]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]]
